body:
## Summary
- Parse planner `user_message` and `clarification_request` fields for blocked decisions.
- Post blocked planner messages as visible terminal clarification comments without treating `answer` as a handoff action.
- Update orchestrator prompt/docs and focused regression coverage.

## Tests
- `npm run build`
- `env -u BASE_BRANCH node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/handoff.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #105

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777776732415 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjgwMTk1ODM6aW1wbGVtZW50OjEwNTpyZXZpZXc6MTEzOjM -->

The linked issue confirms the intended design: blocked planner output should ask a visible question and pause, not add `answer` as a dispatchable action. I‚Äôm mapping that against the selected rubrics now; most applicable checks are workflow shape, docs sync, explicit fields, and stopping visibly without unintended automation.## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds separate planner fields `user_message` and `clarification_request` in `.agent/src/handoff.ts` and `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md`, instead of overloading `next_action` or dispatching `answer`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The patch follows issue #105‚Äôs stated design: terminal clarification path, keep `answer` out of orchestrator actions, pause the chain. It also builds on child #95‚Äôs terminal stop comment surface. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are limited to orchestrator planner parsing, handoff comment formatting, prompt/docs, and focused regression tests. No unrelated refactors or broad rewrites are present. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing `PlannerDecision`, `HandoffDecision`, `decideHandoff`, `createOrchestrateStopComment`, and stop-marker comment flow rather than adding a parallel workflow or new dispatch path. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` and `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` to document blocked planner clarification behavior and the `answer` non-handoff rule. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Adds `formatPlannerClarificationComment` / `commentOnPlannerClarificationStop` so blocked planner stops post a visible terminal clarification comment with reason and no-dispatch status. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | The prompt explicitly says not to use `answer` as `next_action`; the CLI test asserts no workflow dispatch and `dispatchPayload` remains `null` for blocked clarification. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric alignment is that the implementation chooses a visible terminal clarification comment instead of expanding the orchestrator action set. That matches the linked issue‚Äôs design and avoids creating a broader delegated route surface.
- The regression coverage is focused on the changed contract: parsing planner fields, carrying them through `decideHandoff`, and verifying the CLI posts the clarification while dispatching no follow-up workflow.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. The selected applicable rubrics are satisfied by the PR diff and linked issue context.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #113 addresses #105 by letting the orchestrator surface planner clarification prompts when automation is blocked.
- It adds explicit `user_message` and `clarification_request` fields, keeps `answer` out of the dispatchable action set, and posts a visible terminal stop comment instead of silently stopping.
- The implementation updates the prompt, technical docs, handoff parsing, stop-comment formatting, and focused tests.
- This fits the broader orchestrator plan: ask the user for missing context, pause, and let the user explicitly resume.

## Review
The reviewers mostly agree the design and implementation are correct. One reviewer found a focused regression-test gap for the fallback path where a blocked planner response omits both user-facing message fields.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Missing fallback test for blocked planner responses without message fields | WARNING | The new golden-path test covers `blocked` with both clarification fields, but not `blocked` with only a `reason`. That fallback should still post the generic stop comment and avoid dispatching a workflow. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific findings.
- One code review found no blocking, warning, or info findings.
- A single inline comment was posted on `.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts` line 848 for the missing fallback coverage.
- The repeated formatter-call concern from one reviewer is informational only and does not require branch work.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Missing fallback test for blocked planner responses without message fields</summary>

**Cause:** `formatPlannerClarificationComment()` returns `null` when the planner decision is `blocked` but both `userMessage` and `clarificationRequest` are empty. The current behavior appears correct because the generic orchestrator stop comment should still be posted, but this fallback is not covered by the new tests.

**Candidate solutions:** Add an adjacent CLI regression test where `FAKE_PLANNER_RESPONSE` is `{"decision":"blocked","reason":"Context missing."}`. Assert that the generic `Sepo orchestration stopped after` comment posts, the clarification comment does not post, and no workflow dispatch occurs.

**Comments:** Reviewers did not disagree on behavior. The disagreement is only priority: two reviews found the PR shippable as-is, while one reviewer recommended locking down this fallback before merge.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The only unresolved finding is a small, concrete regression test that is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add regression coverage for `blocked` planner responses that omit `user_message` and `clarification_request`, asserting the generic stop comment still posts and no workflow dispatch occurs.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

review verdict is minor_issues; dispatching fix-pr

- Added regression coverage for blocked planner responses without `user_message` or `clarification_request`, asserting the generic stop comment posts and no workflow dispatch occurs.
- Verified with `npm run build`, focused handoff/envelope tests, and `git diff --check`.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 5/10).

PR fixes succeeded; dispatching review

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The planner contract adds separate `user_message` and `clarification_request` fields in [`handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/da15c596e88ac189b84c0fc78d37e5c4e4b5a53b/.agent/src/handoff.ts) and the orchestrator prompt, rather than overloading `next_action` or encoding clarification in a command string. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR follows issue #105‚Äôs accepted design: use `blocked` plus user-facing clarification fields, keep `answer` out of the orchestrator action set, and pause. It also builds on child #95‚Äôs terminal stop comment surface. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to planner parsing, handoff stop-comment formatting, focused tests, and docs/prompt updates across seven files. No unrelated refactor or broad cleanup is present. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation extends existing `PlannerDecision`, `HandoffDecision`, `decideHandoff`, `createOrchestrateStopComment`, and orchestrate stop marker flow instead of adding a parallel workflow or new dispatch path. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/da15c596e88ac189b84c0fc78d37e5c4e4b5a53b/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md) and [`agent-orchestrator.md` prompt](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/da15c596e88ac189b84c0fc78d37e5c4e4b5a53b/.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md) to document blocked clarification behavior and the `answer` non-handoff rule. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/da15c596e88ac189b84c0fc78d37e5c4e4b5a53b/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts) now formats blocked planner decisions as visible terminal clarification comments with reason, round, target, and no-dispatch status. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | The prompt explicitly says not to use `answer` as `next_action`, avoiding expansion of delegated orchestrator routes beyond the existing authorized action set. Dispatch remains limited to existing validated handoff paths. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | Tests assert blocked clarification stops do not dispatch a workflow and leave `dispatchPayload` as `null`; the follow-up fix added coverage for blocked planner responses without message fields falling back to a generic stop comment. |

## Notes

- The PR aligns well with the selected rubrics because it solves the UX gap with a terminal clarification stop, not by broadening the orchestrator into an `answer` dispatcher.
- The current head includes the follow-up regression test requested by prior review: blocked planner output with only `reason` posts the generic stop comment and dispatches no workflow.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. The selected applicable rubrics are satisfied by the current PR diff, linked issue context, docs updates, and focused regression coverage.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #113 implements issue #105 by letting the orchestrator ask for missing user context through blocked planner decisions.
- It adds explicit `user_message` and `clarification_request` fields, surfaces them in a visible terminal stop comment, and keeps `answer` out of the dispatchable handoff action set.
- This fits the broader #104 stacked orchestrator UX plan and builds on #112‚Äôs parent terminal stop-comment surface.
- The PR also updates prompt/docs and adds focused regression coverage for clarification and generic fallback stop behavior.

## Review
The reviewers agree the current head is ready to ship. The prior warning about missing fallback coverage was addressed, and the remaining reviewer notes are optional INFO-level observations that do not require branch work.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues remain | INFO | Codex, Claude, and the rubrics review found no unresolved required changes after the follow-up fix added the missing blocked-without-message fallback coverage. |

## Progress
- Earlier synthesis identified missing fallback coverage for blocked planner responses without message fields.
- Fix-pr added the fallback test asserting the generic stop comment posts and no workflow dispatch occurs.
- Rubrics review passed 100/100 across the applicable workflow and coding rubrics.
- No new inline comments were posted; the only prior inline test-gap comment is already addressed on the current PR head.
- Optional notes about repeated pure formatter calls and an additional parser-unit test were treated as non-blocking and left out of automation action items.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved actionable branch work remains, so the PR is ready for human merge review.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

title: Surface orchestrator clarification prompts
